entries.title: 0L Petrol
entries.history: Entry-level 100 PS EcoBoost code for early B-Max models; wet belt design brought efficiency but also timing failure risks.
compatibleVariants: Studio, Zetec, Cool & Connect, SYNC Edition
commonFailures: Wet timing belt degradation causing oil pickup blockage (60-80k miles)
Turbo oil seal failure secondary to oil starvation
Coolant loss leading to head gasket stress

entries.title: 0L Petrol

commonFailures: Wet belt fibre shedding clogging oil strainer (60-80k miles)
Oil pressure warning light triggered by blockage
Misfire codes P0300-P0303 from timing correlation loss

entries.title: 0L Petrol

compatibleVariants: Zetec, Titanium, Cool & Connect, SYNC Edition
commonFailures: Timing belt degradation in oil causing starvation (60-80k miles)
Turbocharger failure from contaminated lubrication
Cranks but won't start due to belt jump or breakage

entries.title: 0L Petrol

commonFailures: Wet timing belt failure causing oil pickup blockage (60-80k miles)
Oil pressure warning followed by engine seizure risk
Turbo failure secondary to oil starvation from belt debris

commonFailures: Oil-immersed timing belt degradation (60-80k miles)

Rattling noise from belt tensioner or chain guide wear

entries.title: 0L Petrol

entries.title: 4L Petrol

commonProblems.h2: Ford B-Max Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Ford B-Max is generally reliable, but its 1.0 EcoBoost, 1.6 Duratec, and 1.5/1.6 TDCi engines each carry specific failure points that commonly trigger replacement searches. The 1.0 EcoBoost’s oil-immersed timing belt is the most documented issue, while diesel variants frequently suffer from DPF blockages and injector wear. Every rebuilt Ford B-Max engine supplied through our network includes upgraded internal components designed to eliminate these original factory weaknesses, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the breakdowns below to compare repair costs against full replacement and decide the most cost‑effective path for your vehicle.

problems.group: Ford B-Max 1.0 EcoBoost Wet Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Ford B-Max 1.0 EcoBoost Wet Timing Belt Failure - Why Early 2012-2015 Units Degrade Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Ford B-Max Zetec, Titanium, Cool & Connect, SYNC Edition (2012-2017, SFJA, SFJB, M1JA)
problems.rootCause: The timing belt runs inside the oil sump rather than externally. Over time, the rubber degrades, sheds fibres that clog the oil pickup strainer, and causes catastrophic oil starvation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing belt, tensioner, and water pump replacement before fibre shedding occurs.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory spec. Highly recommended if belt is intact and mileage is under 70k.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine sump removal, oil pickup strainer cleaning, turbo seal replacement, and fresh oil system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if belt hasn't fully failed and no internal bearing damage exists.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit of a reconditioned unit with upgraded timing belt guides, reinforced strainer, and bench-tested oil pressure system.
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution. Addresses root cause permanently with 12‑month warranty included.

problems.recommendation: If your Ford B-Max is still running without oil pressure warnings, act immediately with a preventative belt change. Once the oil pickup clogs or the engine seizes, repair costs quickly exceed the car’s market value. At 70,000+ miles with belt debris in the sump, a full replacement is almost always more economical and reliable than attempting a partial clean-out.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt SFJA/M1JA 1.0 EcoBoost Ford B-Max engine prices

problems.h4: Ford B-Max 1.0 EcoBoost Turbo Failure - The Secondary Effect of Oil Starvation
problems.affectedModels: Ford B-Max Titanium, Individual (2012-2017, M1JA, YYJC)
problems.rootCause: Degraded wet timing belt particles contaminate engine oil, starving the turbocharger bearings of lubrication and causing shaft seizure or seal blowout.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Refurbishment & Oil Line Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo core refurbishment, new oil feed/return lines, and system flush to remove belt debris.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix. High recurrence risk if underlying belt/oil strainer issue remains.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Upgraded Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a complete rebuilt engine featuring reinforced oil passages, new turbo assembly, and corrected wet belt geometry.
repairOptions.longevity: Eliminates both the turbo failure and the original belt design flaw. 12‑month warranty applies.

problems.recommendation: A standalone turbo replacement on a Ford B-Max with known wet belt contamination rarely solves the underlying problem. The contaminated oil will quickly destroy a new turbo unit. We recommend full engine replacement once turbo whine, blue smoke, or power loss appears alongside timing belt warnings.
problems.cta: Get instant quotes for YYJC/M1JA Ford B-Max turbo failure repair or replacement

problems.group: Ford B-Max 1.5/1.6 TDCi DPF & Injector Failure
problems.h4: Ford B-Max 1.5/1.6 TDCi DPF & Injector Failure - Why Urban Driving Kills Diesel Engines
problems.affectedModels: Ford B-Max Studio, Zetec, Titanium, Cool & Connect (2012-2017, T3JB, UGJC)
problems.rootCause: Short urban trips prevent DPF regeneration cycles, causing ash buildup and backpressure. This stresses high-pressure fuel injectors, leading to seal leaks and combustion chamber washdown.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Professional DPF chemical cleaning, injector leak-off test, and ECU recalibration.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective only if injectors are not mechanically failed and DPF structure is intact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: OEM-spec injector replacement, new DPF unit, EGR valve service, and full fuel system pressure test.
repairOptions.longevity: Restores performance but leaves aging diesel ancillaries at risk of future failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete supply & fit of a rebuilt diesel unit with refurbished injectors, cleared oil galleries, and updated ECU mapping.
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable long-term fix for high-mileage diesels. 12‑month warranty included.

problems.recommendation: If your Ford B-Max diesel is used primarily for town driving, DPF issues are almost inevitable. Cleaning works only as a short-term patch. When injector seal failure coincides with heavy DPF blockage, a complete rebuilt diesel engine offers better value than piecemeal OEM part replacements, especially given current main dealer labour rates.
problems.cta: Compare T3JB/UGJC Ford B-Max diesel engine replacement prices

problems.group: Ford B-Max 1.6 Duratec Timing Chain Stretch & Oil Consumption
problems.h4: Ford B-Max 1.6 Duratec Timing Chain Stretch & Oil Consumption - When Maintenance Is Too Late
problems.affectedModels: Ford B-Max Zetec, Titanium, Individual (2012-2017, XTJA)
problems.rootCause: The timing chain tensioner relies on consistent oil pressure. As piston rings wear and oil consumption increases, reduced pressure allows the chain to stretch and jump timing marks, causing valve interference.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Timing Chain Kit & Tensioner Service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New chain, guides, hydraulic tensioner, and oil pump replacement with full system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if performed before the chain jumps timing or damages valves.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of a fully rebuilt unit with upgraded chain components, reinforced piston rings, and new cylinder head gasket.
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent resolution for high-mileage 1.6L units. Includes 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.recommendation: The 1.6 Duratec is generally robust, but timing chain failure is often preceded by excessive oil consumption and a distinct rattling noise on cold starts. If your Ford B-Max still has stable compression and no valve damage, a preventative chain kit replacement is highly cost-effective. Once the timing jumps, a full rebuild or replacement becomes unavoidable.
problems.cta: Compare XTJA 1.6 Duratec Ford B-Max engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford B-Max
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you’re dealing with wet belt debris on a 1.0 EcoBoost, DPF strain on a TDCi diesel, or timing chain wear on a 1.6 Duratec, piecemeal repairs rarely match the reliability of a complete unit swap. Our network connects you directly with vetted UK specialists who supply fully rebuilt Ford B-Max engines with upgraded components, nationwide delivery, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Stop comparing fragmented repair quotes and secure a transparent, all-inclusive supply & fit package that gets your B-Max back on the road for less than main dealer pricing.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford B-Max Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket’s UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford B-Max and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Ford B-Max Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement officially fitted to the Ford B-Max is listed below with matching engine codes, production years and verified failure notes to ensure accurate replacement matching.

items.title: Ford B-Max 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced in late 2013, the 1.5-litre TDCi replaced the older 1.6 unit in later B-Max production runs to meet stricter Euro emissions standards while maintaining strong low-end torque for urban driving. This compact Duratorq engine became the standard diesel choice for mid-to-high spec trims, offering improved real-world fuel economy without sacrificing daily drivability.
commonFailurePoints: Diesel particulate filter (DPF) blockage from short journeys
injector seal leaks causing oil contamination
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford B-Max 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Ford B-Max 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The original 1.6-litre TDCi served as the sole diesel option during the B-Max’s launch in 2012, utilising a proven Ford-PSA joint venture design known for durability and straightforward mechanical maintenance. It delivers reliable torque across early model years but requires attentive servicing to prevent emissions-related complications common to this generation of compact diesel units.
commonFailurePoints: EGR valve carbon buildup restricting airflow
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford B-Max 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Ford B-Max 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.0-litre three-cylinder EcoBoost became the defining engine for the B-Max, dominating UK registrations with its lightweight aluminium block, direct injection and turbocharging technology. Offered across 100, 120 and 140 PS tunes, it delivers strong performance and tax-friendly efficiency but requires strict adherence to service intervals due to its unique oil-immersed timing belt architecture.
commonFailurePoints: Wet timing belt degradation causing oil pickup strainer blockage
items.cta: Compare Ford B-Max 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford B-Max 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The naturally aspirated 1.4-litre Duratec served as the entry-level petrol option for early B-Max models, prioritising mechanical simplicity and lower initial ownership costs over outright performance. While less common than the EcoBoost units, it appeals to budget-conscious buyers seeking a reliable, low-complexity replacement without turbocharging or direct injection dependencies.
commonFailurePoints: Oil sludge accumulation from extended service intervals
ignition coil degradation causing cylinder misfires
items.cta: Compare Ford B-Max 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford B-Max 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: Positioned as the mid-range petrol alternative, the 1.6-litre Duratec provided smooth power delivery and was the only petrol unit officially paired with the Powershift automatic transmission. Its conventional overhead-cam design avoids the complexity of the smaller turbocharged units, making it a straightforward choice for owners prioritising predictable maintenance over peak efficiency. Not sure which engine size your Ford B-Max needs? Enter your registration number above - we’ll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford B-Max specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear leading to rattling on cold starts
items.cta: Compare Ford B-Max 1.6L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Ford B-Max Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline maps every engine change across the 2012-2017 production run so owners can quickly identify their unit and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The B-Max debuted on the updated Fiesta platform, introducing the 1.0 EcoBoost to a mainstream UK mini-MPV for the first time.
keyChanges: Launch of the sliding-door mini MPV body style in the UK market
Introduction of the 1.0-litre three-cylinder EcoBoost petrol family
Availability of traditional 1.4L and 1.6L naturally aspirated Duratec engines
Initial 1.6 TDCi diesel option carried over from the outgoing Fiesta/Focus platform
knownFor: High initial demand for the 120PS EcoBoost in Titanium trim
Wet timing belt design introduced without widespread owner awareness of maintenance requirements
T3JB 1.6 TDCi established as a reliable motorway cruiser with strong low-end torque
Early build quality issues with door sliding mechanisms overshadowing initial engine reliability

years.description: Ford updated the diesel lineup with a more efficient 1.5L unit and introduced mid-spec trims to broaden the model's urban appeal.
keyChanges: Phased introduction of the 1.5 TDCi UGJC engine to replace the aging 1.6 TDCi
Launch of the Cool & Connect trim targeting urban and fleet buyers
Minor ECU recalibrations for the 1.0 EcoBoost to improve real-world fuel economy
SYNC Edition trim added with upgraded infotainment across the mid-range
knownFor: UGJC 1.5 TDCi resolved some early DPF clogging complaints but introduced new high-pressure injector sensitivity
Peak registration years for the 1.0 EcoBoost, creating sustained replacement demand today
XTJA timing chain tensioner wear becomes noticeable on higher-mileage automatic variants
Strong secondary market value retention compared to rival superminis of the era

years.description: The final production years saw the addition of a higher-output 140PS EcoBoost and the premium Individual trim before the model was discontinued.
keyChanges: Introduction of the YYJC 1.0 EcoBoost 140PS engine for top-tier specifications
Launch of the Individual trim featuring sport suspension and upgraded interior materials
Discontinuation of the 1.4L and 1.6L naturally aspirated petrol options
End of UK production in 2017 with final clearance stock and warranty extensions
popularModels: Ford B-Max Titanium SYNC Edition 1.0 EcoBoost 120PS

faq.h2: Ford B-Max Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Ford B-Max?
items.answer: Yes, if your Ford B-Max is in good condition otherwise. A rebuilt Ford B-Max engine costs £1,200-£2,500 supply-only, far less than buying a replacement vehicle. Most owners recover 2-3 more years of reliable use. Compare quotes before deciding to scrap.
keyPoints: Ford B-Max values range £1,500-£4,000 depending on trim and mileage
Rebuilt engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists
Supply and fit packages typically cost £2,000-£3,500 all-in for Ford B-Max
EcoBoost 1.0 units dominate replacement demand due to wet belt issues
items.cta: Get instant Ford B-Max engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: What causes Ford B-Max 1.0 EcoBoost timing belt failure?
items.answer: The Ford B-Max 1.0 EcoBoost uses an oil-immersed wet timing belt that degrades over time. Fibres shed into the oil, clogging the pickup strainer and causing oil starvation. This leads to turbo failure or complete engine seizure if ignored on your Ford B-Max.
keyPoints: Wet belt degradation typically occurs between 60,000-90,000 miles on Ford B-Max
SFJA, M1JA and YYJC engine codes all share this wet belt design
Oil pressure warning lights often appear just before catastrophic Ford B-Max failure
Regular oil changes with correct specification can extend belt life slightly
items.cta: Compare rebuilt SFJA M1JA Ford B-Max EcoBoost engine prices now

items.question: How much does a Ford B-Max engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Ford B-Max engine replacement costs £1,200-£2,500 for supply-only rebuilt units, or £2,000-£3,500 for supply and fit packages. Used engines start around £950 but carry shorter warranties. Prices vary by engine code, variant and supplier location across the UK.
keyPoints: 1.0 EcoBoost rebuilt engines typically cost £1,200-£2,200 supply-only for Ford B-Max
Diesel T3JB and UGJC units range £1,300-£2,300 depending on condition
Supply and fit labour adds £600-£850 to your Ford B-Max engine price
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty coverage
items.cta: Compare live Ford B-Max engine replacement prices from UK suppliers today

items.question: Which Ford B-Max engine is most reliable - petrol or diesel?
items.answer: The Ford B-Max 1.6 Duratec petrol is generally most reliable, avoiding EcoBoost wet belt risks. Diesel TDCi units offer strong economy but suffer DPF issues on short journeys. Choose based on your driving pattern, not just fuel type preference for your Ford B-Max.
keyPoints: 1.6 Duratec XTJA code has simpler timing chain design on Ford B-Max
1.0 EcoBoost offers best performance but requires strict wet belt maintenance
T3JB and UGJC diesel Ford B-Max engines need regular motorway runs
All Ford B-Max engines benefit from full service history and correct oil specs
items.cta: Find reliable Ford B-Max petrol or diesel engine options with warranty

items.question: How do I find my Ford B-Max engine code?
items.answer: Your Ford B-Max engine code appears on the VIN plate under the bonnet, on the cambelt cover, or in your V5C logbook section D. Common codes include SFJA, M1JA, YYJC for EcoBoost, and T3JB, UGJC for diesel Ford B-Max models.
keyPoints: Check the engine bay VIN plate for your Ford B-Max code stamp
Enter your registration online to instantly verify Ford B-Max engine details
EcoBoost codes SFJA SFJB M1JA YYJC all fit different Ford B-Max years
Diesel codes T3JB (1.6L) and UGJC (1.5L) suit specific Ford B-Max variants
items.cta: Enter your reg to identify your exact Ford B-Max engine code instantly

items.question: Can I replace my Ford B-Max engine with a used one?
items.answer: Yes, used Ford B-Max engines are available from £950-£1,800 supply-only. They offer budget replacement but carry shorter warranties than rebuilt units. Always verify mileage, compression test results and warranty terms before purchasing a used Ford B-Max engine.
keyPoints: Used Ford B-Max engines typically come with 3-6 month warranties only
Request compression test reports before buying any used Ford B-Max engine
EcoBoost used units should include oil pickup strainer inspection confirmation
Nationwide delivery available for used Ford B-Max engines from verified breakers
items.cta: Check availability of used Ford B-Max engines with warranty coverage now

items.question: What are the signs of Ford B-Max EcoBoost oil pressure failure?
items.answer: Warning signs include oil pressure light illumination, rattling noise on cold start, loss of power, or the Ford B-Max cranking but not starting. These indicate wet belt debris has blocked the oil pickup, requiring immediate engine inspection or replacement.
keyPoints: Oil pressure warning on Ford B-Max EcoBoost demands immediate engine shutdown
Rattling from timing cover suggests belt degradation on your Ford B-Max
Blue smoke or turbo whistle indicates secondary turbo damage in Ford B-Max
Misfire codes P0016 P0017 often precede total Ford B-Max EcoBoost failure
items.cta: Get urgent quotes for Ford B-Max EcoBoost engine replacement from UK specialists

items.question: How long does a Ford B-Max engine replacement take?
items.answer: Ford B-Max engine supply typically takes 1-3 working days from UK stock. Professional fitting requires 1-2 full days depending on garage workload. Supply and fit packages coordinate both stages, getting your Ford B-Max back on the road in 3-5 days total.
keyPoints: Most Ford B-Max engines are held in UK stock for rapid dispatch
EcoBoost replacements may require ECU coding by Ford B-Max specialists
Supply and fit packages include old engine collection from your location
Warranty activation begins once your Ford B-Max replacement engine is fitted
items.cta: Find Ford B-Max engine supply and fit deals with fast turnaround

items.question: Are Ford B-Max diesel engines prone to DPF problems?
items.answer: Yes, Ford B-Max 1.5 TDCi and 1.6 TDCi engines can suffer DPF blockage if used mainly for short urban journeys. Symptoms include reduced power, frequent regeneration attempts, and limp mode. Regular motorway driving helps, but severe blockages may require Ford B-Max engine replacement.
keyPoints: DPF issues affect UGJC and T3JB diesel Ford B-Max engines most commonly
Injector seal leaks can worsen DPF problems on your Ford B-Max diesel
Professional DPF cleaning costs £300-£600 but may not resolve Ford B-Max issues
Rebuilt diesel Ford B-Max engines include cleared oil galleries and updated mapping
items.cta: Compare Ford B-Max diesel engine replacement prices for DPF-related failures

items.question: Will a Ford Fiesta engine fit my Ford B-Max?
items.answer: Some Ford B-Max engines share architecture with the Fiesta, particularly the 1.0 EcoBoost family. However, engine codes SFJA M1JA YYJC must match exactly for your Ford B-Max variant. Never assume cross-compatibility - always verify codes before ordering any replacement Ford B-Max engine.
keyPoints: Ford B-Max and Fiesta share 1.0 EcoBoost block design but differ in mounts
ECU calibration and ancillary fittings vary between Ford B-Max and Fiesta engines
Diesel T3JB UGJC codes are specific to Ford B-Max platform applications
Using the wrong Ford B-Max engine code can cause fitment and warranty issues
items.cta: Verify your Ford B-Max engine code compatibility before ordering replacement
